conclusion: Forcing c4 full-attention layer copy and post-attention add/RMSNorm to per-row diagnostics does not change the L4-L8 bridge outcome. Rowchunk2 remains generated-token green with zero linear-projection drift and no full-attention stage failures; rowchunk3 still has layer4 QKV/Z drift at L5, the same L6 truncated-token failure (row1 first mismatch at generated index 4), and the same L8 layer7 attn_input_pre_qkv failure. The grouped>=3 c4 drift is therefore not caused by the layer3 output-to-layer4 copy/RMSNorm handoff; the bad trajectory is already present in the layer3 full-attention output values that feed the next linear layer.
